summary: Updated the GitHub Actions build workflow sparse-checkout lists to include the `extern` directory in two checkout steps. This ensures CI jobs that use sparse checkout have access to external dependency files alongside `src`, `tools`, `config`, and, in one job, `.nix`. The PR was very small, merged quickly, and had no review comments or explanatory body, so the motivation is inferred from the diff and title rather than explicit discussion.
what_changed: Added `extern` to a sparse-checkout block in `.github/workflows/build.yml` near the checkout configured with `fetch-depth: 2`.
Added `extern` to a second sparse-checkout block in `.github/workflows/build.yml` near the checkout configured with `fetch-depth: 1`, before `tools`, `config`, and `.nix`.
No source code, headers, structs, or decompilation functions were changed.
smart_moves: Kept the CI checkout minimal while expanding it only enough to include `extern`, preserving the intended sparse-checkout behavior instead of disabling sparse checkout entirely.
Applied the same directory addition to both relevant checkout configurations, avoiding a partial CI fix where one build path succeeds and another still lacks external files.
decomp_lessons: When CI uses sparse checkout, repository directories required by builds or tooling must be explicitly listed; adding dependencies under `extern` requires updating CI checkout filters as well as local build assumptions.

For future decomp work, changes that introduce or rely on external assets, libraries, generated includes, or tooling inputs under `extern` should be checked against GitHub Actions sparse-checkout configuration.
Small infrastructure fixes can unblock matching/build work even without touching decompilation source; keep CI path filters synchronized with repository layout.
naming_conventions: The repository directory is named `extern`; this PR reinforces that CI should refer to it directly as a top-level sparse-checkout entry.
No symbol, function, struct, or header naming conventions were discussed.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly matching tactics were present; this was a CI sparse-checkout update only.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that add files under `extern` or depend on `extern` in CI builds.
Search for other sparse-checkout blocks in workflows that may need top-level repository directories added.
Check whether CI failures before PR 1412 referenced missing `extern` files or dependencies.
